item.title: Video appears to show United employee threatening to call ICE on customer
item.description: A United Airlines employee appears to have been caught on video threatening to call ICE on a passenger last week at San Francisco International Airport.

item.title: Iran attacks U.S. allies as 3 American soldiers killed in escalating war
item.description: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as at least 3 U.S. forces are killed and dozens wounded in the escalating conflict.

item.title: Deadly attack on U.S. forces in Jordan part of evolving Iranian tactics
item.description: A retired Jordanian general tells CBS News Iran is "developing techniques and capacities" to target U.S. forces with increasing lethality.

item.title: Hegseth, Caine to testify before Senate panel amid escalating Iran war
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will testify before a Senate committee amid escalations in the war with Iran in recent days.

item.title: Trump admin. halts $1 billion in Medicaid payments to California, Minnesota
item.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said his agency is pausing $867.5 million in Medicaid payments to California and $199 million to Minnesota.

item.title: Key Arizona primaries to watch today
item.description: Arizona has traditionally been a red state, and six of its nine House representatives are Republicans.

item.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

item.title: Woman attacked by bear while hiking with friend and dogs in Alaska
item.description: A woman suffered injuries after being attacked by a brown bear in southern Alaska, where she was out hiking with a friend and three dogs, authorities said.

item.title: What's in Republicans' $95 billion budget resolution?
item.description: The Republicans' budget resolution includes $60 billion for the Pentagon, $13 billion for national intelligence, $12 billion in farm assistance and $10 billion for elections, including grants for states adopting President Trump's stricter voter ID laws. CBS News' Bradley Blackburn breaks it down.

item.title: Facing a debt collection lawsuit? Here's what to do in the next 30 days.
item.description: A debt collection lawsuit doesn't have to catch you off guard. Here's how to protect yourself in the first 30 days.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens, threatening Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha threatens Gulf Coast with flooding and storm surge
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha formed on Monday, threatening the Gulf Coast. Meanwhile in the Midwest, severe storms caused flooding in Des Moines, Iowa, and several tornadoes were spotted in northern Wisconsin. Lana Zak reports.

item.title: Appeals court denies Biden bid to block release of tapes with biographer, for now
item.description: An appeals court denied former President Joe Biden's attempt to keep the Justice Department from giving a conservative think tank redacted transcripts and audio recordings of conversations with his biographer.

item.title: What happens to joint credit cards after one spouse dies?
item.description: One spouse dies, but what happens to the shared credit card accounts? The answer isn't always straightforward.

item.title: Tourists stabbed near famed Acropolis in Athens, Greece
item.description: A man stabbed two tourists near the ancient Acropolis in Athens, Greece's top tourist attraction, police said.

item.title: 2 American tourists stabbed near Acropolis in Athens, Greece
item.description: Two Greek American tourists were stabbed near the Acropolis in Athens, Greece, police said. CBS News' Anna Coren has more.

item.title: Key Arizona primary races to watch
item.description: Arizona voters head to the polls Tuesday for some key primary races, including the GOP contest for governor and House District 1. CBS News' Taurean Small breaks it down.

item.title: Questions remain on charges, motive of suspect accused of setting fire outside NYC building
item.description: More details are emerging about the Army veteran accused of starting a fire outside a federal building in lower Manhattan on Monday. CBS News' Tom Hanson has more.

item.title: U.S. hits Iran with strikes for 10th night amid reports of injured service members
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran for a tenth night as CBS News reports that nearly 100 service members have been injured on multiple bases in the Middle East this month. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Aaron Navarro have more.

item.title: Iran war has "real human consequences," Marine Corps veteran says
item.description: Nearly 100 service members have been injured in Iranian airstrikes on U.S. bases in the Middle East this month, officials told CBS News. Kyleanne Hunter, the CEO of Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans of America and a Marine Corps veteran, joins "CBS News Mornings" to discuss the toll of the Iran war on service members.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Josh Duhamel talks "Ransom Canyon" series and opens up about his growing family
item.description: Josh Duhamel talks with "CBS Mornings" about starring in "Ransom Canyon," how much he knew about ranch life before the show and his growing family.

item.title: Younger Democrats push for committee leadership term limits, generational change
item.description: A group of younger Democrats is pushing for term limits for committee leadership roles, citing gerontocracy as a major issue hindering American democracy. Majority Democrats' Rodericka Applewhaite explains why she believes age should carry less weight when considering political leadership roles.

item.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, accusing it of false advertising
item.description: The lawsuit alleges that Eli Lilly misled consumers by using outdated clinical trial data to make its drugs appear more effective than Novo's.

item.title: 2 soldiers from Texas and Hawaii killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 388,000 vehicles over seat issue
item.description: The recall affects certain 2020-2027 Aviator and 2020-2026 Explorer vehicles.

item.title: What to know about Taylor Farms amid the cyclospora outbreak
item.description: The nation's largest recent cyclospora outbreak has put one of America's biggest produce suppliers under renewed scrutiny.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, says she'll run for full Senate term
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham, who is serving out the remainder of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's term in the Senate, said she will seek a full term for the South Carolina seat her brother held for more than two decades.

item.title: Mark Warner says he'll vote against Trump pick Jay Clayton as DNI
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner said he'll vote against Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence, citing disappointment in his testimony at a confirmation hearing last week despite his previous optimism about the nominee.

item.title: 2 families sue hospital after Colorado man claims he was switched at birth
item.description: Two years ago, Jeremy Morrison took a DNA test and found out his parents were not biological actual parents.
